---
title: "Assignment 1"
---

This assignment consists of two main parts: a video summary with related questions and a set of R programming tasks performed on a specific dataset.

## A. **Video Summary**

Video Title: [Veri Bilimi ve Endüstri Mühendisliği Üzerine Sohbetler - Mustafa Baydoğan & Erdi Daşdemir](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1Mvkn71dhaA)

### **Overview**

The video discusses the relationship between Industrial Engineering and Data Science and explains how these two fields work together to solve real-world problems. The speaker emphasizes that data should not be analyzed alone. Instead, engineers should understand the system, process, or business environment behind the data. Industrial Engineers are well prepared for this because their education includes mathematics, statistics, optimization, and problem-solving methods. These skills help them analyze complex systems and make decisions based on data.

Another important point in the video is that prediction is only one part of the process. In many business situations, the main goal is not only to forecast what will happen but also to decide what action should be taken. For this reason, the lecture highlights the importance of Prescriptive Analytics, which combines prediction with optimization and decision making. This approach helps companies choose the best strategy based on available data and possible future scenarios.

The video also presents several practical examples to explain how data science is used in different industries. One example focuses on the manufacturing sector, where engineers predict whether wood will bend during production. By identifying this problem early, companies can take preventive actions and reduce financial losses. Another example comes from the energy sector, where electricity production and consumption must be balanced at all times. Accurate demand forecasts are important because incorrect predictions can lead to significant economic costs.

In addition, the lecture discusses how data science is used in online retail platforms. Companies analyze customer behavior, product popularity, and inventory information to decide how products should be ranked on websites. The goal is to show customers the most relevant products while also maximizing sales and managing stock levels effectively.

Finally, the speaker explains that in many business environments, models should be understandable and explainable. Even if complex machine learning models can achieve high accuracy, companies often prefer simpler models that can be clearly explained to managers or decision makers. The lecture also highlights that learning data science requires practical experience. Working with real, imperfect datasets and solving real problems is essential for developing strong analytical skills.

### **Summary**

-   Industrial Engineers combine data analysis, optimization, and decision making.

-   Data science is applied in manufacturing, energy markets, and online retail.

-   Prescriptive Analytics helps decide the best action based on predictions.

-   In business, interpretable models are often preferred.

-   Practical experience with real data is essential to learn data science.

### **Questions & Answers**

1.  Why is "interpretability" considered a priority in Industrial Engineering data science projects compared to using "black-box" models?

***Answer:*** Interpretability is vital because business decisions often require justification for stakeholders or regulatory compliance. If a model's logic is hidden, it becomes nearly impossible to perform root cause analysis or build trust with decision-makers when errors occur.

2.  According to the video, which type of analytics focuses on "What should we do?" by combining predictions with optimization?

<!-- -->

A)  Descriptive Analytics

B)  Predictive Analytics

C)  Prescriptive Analytics

D)  Diagnostic Analytics

**Answer: C) Prescriptive Analytics**

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## B. Assignment 

## Import the data

---
library(dslabs)
data("polls_us_election_2016")

# describe variables
my_first <- "Tarık"
my_birth_year <- 2003

# Calculate k value
k <- (nchar(my_first) + my_birth_year) %% 15 + 8
print(paste("calculated k value:", k))
---

-   Hesaplanan K değeri: 21

## Original dataset (first k column / last k column)

---
if (k %% 2 == 0) { output_original <- head(polls_us_election_2016, k) } else { output_original <- tail(polls_us_election_2016, k) }
print(output_original)
---

```         
              state  startdate    enddate                pollster grade 4188 South Carolina 2016-09-23 2016-10-06                   Ipsos    A- 4189           Utah 2016-10-25 2016-10-31 Google Consumer Surveys     B 4190  New Hampshire 2016-01-04 2016-01-06   Public Policy Polling    B+ 4191        Florida 2016-06-08 2016-06-19   Quinnipiac University    A- 4192           Ohio 2016-06-08 2016-06-19   Quinnipiac University    A- 4193       New York 2016-02-28 2016-03-03           Siena College     A 4194 North Carolina 2016-09-27 2016-10-02   Quinnipiac University    A- 4195 North Carolina 2016-02-14 2016-02-16               SurveyUSA     A 4196 North Carolina 2016-05-20 2016-05-22   Public Policy Polling    B+ 4197       Kentucky 2016-09-30 2016-10-13                   Ipsos    A- 4198        Florida 2016-07-30 2016-08-07   Quinnipiac University    A- 4199   Pennsylvania 2016-06-08 2016-06-19   Quinnipiac University    A- 4200           Ohio 2016-06-30 2016-07-11   Quinnipiac University    A- 4201 North Carolina 2016-03-18 2016-03-20   Public Policy Polling    B+ 4202   South Dakota 2016-10-28 2016-11-02                   Ipsos    A- 4203     Washington 2016-10-21 2016-11-02                   Ipsos    A- 4204       Virginia 2016-09-16 2016-09-22                   Ipsos    A- 4205      Wisconsin 2016-08-04 2016-08-07    Marquette University     A 4206           Utah 2016-11-01 2016-11-07 Google Consumer Surveys     B 4207         Oregon 2016-10-21 2016-11-02                   Ipsos    A- 4208       Michigan 2016-01-23 2016-01-26                EPIC-MRA    A-      samplesize population rawpoll_clinton rawpoll_trump 4188        258         lv           46.67         48.25 4189        245         lv           28.18         29.39 4190       1036          v           50.00         36.00 4191        975         rv           42.00         36.00 4192        971         rv           38.00         36.00 4193        800         rv           57.00         34.00 4194        507         lv           46.00         43.00 4195       1250         lv           43.00         45.00 4196        928          v           41.00         43.00 4197        336         lv           39.38         53.08 4198       1056         lv           43.00         43.00 4199        950         rv           39.00         36.00 4200        955         rv           36.00         37.00 4201        843          v           44.00         42.00 4202        170         lv           28.45         47.20 4203        538         lv           46.71         38.33 4204        452         lv           46.54         40.04 4205        683         lv           47.00         34.00 4206        286         lv           21.33         35.05 4207        446         lv           46.46         37.41 4208        600         lv           43.00         41.00      rawpoll_johnson rawpoll_mcmullin adjpoll_clinton adjpoll_trump 4188              NA               NA        46.20387      49.13322 4189            8.40               NA        33.61454      36.01090 4190              NA               NA        47.81869      35.91267 4191            7.00               NA        46.44439      41.33270 4192            8.00               NA        42.46502      41.36491 4193              NA               NA        56.98850      38.83402 4194            7.00               NA        47.79437      44.86935 4195              NA               NA        39.90517      44.40152 4196            3.00               NA        43.28262      47.12021 4197              NA               NA        38.34430      54.36357 4198            7.00               NA        45.19351      46.65680 4199            9.00               NA        43.35339      41.19061 4200            7.00               NA        40.73937      42.33380 4201              NA               NA        42.13165      43.55006 4202              NA               NA        26.57791      45.43384 4203              NA               NA        45.56387      38.22545 4204              NA               NA        46.47852      40.48017 4205            9.00               NA        48.74781      39.07778 4206            9.99               NA        26.65200      40.57738 4207              NA               NA        45.12949      37.10720 4208              NA               NA        42.14966      42.05508      adjpoll_johnson adjpoll_mcmullin 4188              NA               NA 4189        7.458243               NA 4190              NA               NA 4191        2.791570               NA 4192        3.791570               NA 4193              NA               NA 4194        4.022323               NA 4195              NA               NA 4196       -0.036293               NA 4197              NA               NA 4198        3.448447               NA 4199        4.791570               NA 4200        2.936299               NA 4201              NA               NA 4202              NA               NA 4203              NA               NA 4204              NA               NA 4205        4.705020               NA 4206        9.705791               NA 4207              NA               NA 4208              NA               NA
```

# k çift ise ilk k satır, tek ise son k satır

# Toplam NA sayısı

total_na \<- sum(is.na(polls_us_election_2016)) print(paste("Toplam NA sayısı:", total_na))

# Sütun başına NA sayısı (Büyükten küçüğe ilk 8)

na_per_column \<- sort(colSums(is.na(polls_us_election_2016)), decreasing = TRUE) print(head(na_per_column, 8))

# Yeni bir nesne oluştur (orijinali bozmamak için)

cleaned_data \<- polls_us_election_2016

# Döngü ile sütun tiplerine göre doldurma yap

for(i in 1:ncol(cleaned_data)) { if(is.numeric(cleaned_data\[\[i\]\])) { \# Sayısal ise: my_birth_year + k cleaned_data\[\[i\]\]\[is.na(cleaned_data\[\[i\]\])\] \<- my_birth_year + k } else { \# Karakter veya Faktör ise: my_first_k cleaned_data\[\[i\]\] \<- as.character(cleaned_data\[\[i\]\]) \# Faktör hatasını önlemek için cleaned_data\[\[i\]\]\[is.na(cleaned_data\[\[i\]\])\] \<- paste0(my_first, "\_", k) } }

# 1. Yeni veri setinden k kadar satır göster (Aynı kural ile)

if (k %% 2 == 0) { print(head(cleaned_data, k)) } else { print(tail(cleaned_data, k)) }

# 2. Yeni veri setindeki toplam NA sayısı (0 olmalı)

print(paste("Yeni veri setindeki toplam NA:", sum(is.na(cleaned_data))))

# 3. anyNA kontrolü (FALSE dönmeli)

anyNA(cleaned_data)
